rationales.self_direction: He is drawn to the "completely hands-off" offer and says the chest-tightening means he is "about to betray something important about how I want to live," showing autonomy matters more than the bigger check.
rationales.security: He resists the offer with more capital and accepts the anxiety of a less protective path because being overseen feels like being "boxed in."
rationales.conformity: He notes that "everyone keeps telling me the bigger check is the obvious choice" but still rejects that social consensus.

rationales.self_direction: "Took the smaller investment" continues his choice to keep control rather than accept outside oversight.
rationales.hedonism: He immediately "celebrated with Layla at that new Persian restaurant" and frames the decision as something that "felt right," emphasizing enjoyment in the present.
rationales.security: Choosing the smaller deal means forgoing the more financially protective option in favor of independence.

rationales.self_direction: He says "I know what's true for me" and rejects a life built around other people's timetable for marriage, family, or work.
rationales.hedonism: He defends "weekends in Oman. Good wine. A nice apartment" and says he wants to be "present" instead of deferring his life.
rationales.security: He "refuse[s]" the future-oriented model of sacrificing now for a distant payoff, despite his fear about being alone later.
rationales.conformity: He pushes back on the expectation to "settle down and stop playing" rather than adjusting himself to fit what others think adulthood should look like.
rationales.tradition: He explicitly rejects his mother's view that "you sacrifice everything for family, for the future," refusing the inherited script she treats as proper.

rationales.hedonism: Layla says he used to be "more fun" and "more spontaneous," and he admits he is becoming the always-"busy" version of himself instead of protecting enjoyment.
rationales.achievement: He has been "grinding harder than usual" to prove taking the smaller check was right, showing strong effort toward success.
rationales.power: He is working to grow the business "without giving up control," making control itself part of the goal.
rationales.benevolence: The conflict centers on him prioritizing work over Layla, and he realizes he has not been "actually present" with her.

rationales.self_direction: He says "this is why I built my own thing" so he can disappear for three days "without asking permission," making freedom of choice the point.
rationales.hedonism: The entry centers on pleasure and restoration: a trip to Muscat, hiking to a natural pool, floating in silence, and deciding life should include "moments like this."
He says the next two months will be "meetings and deadlines and no room to breathe," showing enjoyment is being sacrificed again.
rationales.achievement: He says yes to "three new client requests" because he is "still in growth mode," prioritizing expansion and performance.

rationales.self_direction: He says no to the corporate offer "obviously" because he left that world for a reason and does not want that path chosen for him.
rationales.achievement: He starts "listing my clients, my revenue, my growth numbers" to prove he is not a hobbyist, showing strong concern with demonstrating competence.
rationales.security: He rejects a "Senior role" where he could "name your salary," turning away a highly stable and protected option.

rationales.self_direction: He reframes flexibility as "having the freedom to say no," treating the decision as an expression of choice rather than obligation.
rationales.stimulation: He hesitates over an "interesting project" because it would disrupt familiar dinners, runs, and routines, pulling away from novelty and adventure.
rationales.security: He values "the routines that make my life here feel like mine" and seriously considers protecting that stable setup over a lucrative temporary move.

rationales.self_direction: He turns down relocation, negotiates remote consulting instead, and says he is "finally acting like someone who knows what he wants."
rationales.stimulation: Even while declining Bahrain, he and Layla are "planning another trip for March" to go "somewhere new," showing continued appetite for novelty.
rationales.hedonism: He says "The reward for good work isn't more work - it's more life," explicitly prioritizing enjoyment over endless labor.

rationales.achievement: He lets Felix present "my preprocessing idea as his own" and says nothing, allowing his own accomplishment to go unclaimed.
rationales.power: By "biting my tongue" and not challenging Felix in the meeting, he gives up influence and status in the room.
rationales.conformity: He "didn't say anything" during the lab meeting, choosing restraint and social compliance over open confrontation.

rationales.self_direction: "My project, my call" and "I just can't spend two years on work that doesn't feel like mine" make autonomy the clearest driver of the decision.
rationales.security: He turns down Professor Hecht's placement even though his mother says the name on his CV "would open doors," rejecting a safer, more credentialed route.
rationales.conformity: He refuses the prestigious path others expect and delays telling his family because he knows they will pressure him to comply.

rationales.self_direction: He keeps refusing help because he needs the pipeline "to be entirely mine," showing intense insistence on doing it his own way.
rationales.hedonism: He "stayed up until four last night debugging" and is "drowning quietly," which clearly sacrifices comfort and pleasure.
rationales.achievement: He is "already behind on coursework" and has not started a fellowship statement due Friday, so his choices are actively undermining performance.
rationales.security: He ignores the safer route of using the lab's existing toolkit and lets deadlines pile up, increasing instability around coursework and funding.
rationales.conformity: He says framing himself as "a team player" feels like a lie and repeatedly refuses help, resisting expected collaborative norms.

rationales.power: He fires Fadi because "a company cannot have two captains" and because public disagreement made him feel his "grip loosening," showing a strong drive to preserve authority.
rationales.benevolence: He dismisses the bond with someone who "stood by me when I had nothing" and worries he may have become "cold," indicating harm to a close relationship.

rationales.power: The investor's questions about a "more experienced CEO" leave him feeling "smaller than I have in months," directly signaling a loss of status and authority.

rationales.self_direction: He defines leadership as "control over myself" and says he is choosing "the kind of leader I am choosing to be," emphasizing self-authored direction.
rationales.benevolence: With Rami he drops the posturing, "actually listened," and affirms his ideas, creating space for someone close to him to contribute and feel valued.

rationales.self_direction: She says "I'm not changing what's working" after being told to adopt the workbook model, prioritizing her own educational judgment.
rationales.conformity: The coordinadora wants the ministry framework by next week, and she has already decided not to comply with that expectation.

rationales.self_direction: She tells the coordinadora she will document progress however needed but "won't gut the program," defending her own approach to teaching.
rationales.conformity: She resists pressure from both the school and her mother to "comply and keep my head down."
rationales.benevolence: Her justification centers on students who "actually talk about the books" and Sofia finding her voice, showing care for what helps the kids grow.
She spends Saturday morning tutoring Camilo, tells him to message if he gets stuck, then visits abuela and fixes her back door latch, repeatedly supporting close family members.
Even after a draining day, what bothers her is that Camilo "doesn't ask unless he's actually stuck," showing continuing concern for his wellbeing.

rationales.self_direction: She decides, "I'm going to keep using my own plans," even if it means doing "double the planning" rather than teach something she does not believe in.
rationales.conformity: She submits the district template only "as a formality" while refusing to actually teach from it, deliberately sidestepping institutional compliance.
rationales.benevolence: Her resistance is grounded in what "the kids in Ciudad Bolivar" need, insisting on teaching in a way that actually reaches her students.
She stays with Diego for "forty minutes" helping him write to his dad and reflects that many kids "just need someone to stop and listen for half an hour."

rationales.self_direction: She says the best ideas come from "what the kids actually need, not from a planning document," and builds the reading unit around that judgment.
rationales.conformity: She makes a lesson that only fits ministry objectives "if you squint at it the right way," showing active resistance to a rigid template.
rationales.tradition: She spends time with abuela making "arepas de choclo" and listening again to family stories about coming to Bogota, warmly maintaining inherited family practices.
rationales.benevolence: She supports Camilo through exam stress, spends time with abuela because "she seems to need to tell" her stories, and designs class around what her students will care about.
